nodes.name: 气候变化
nodes.summary: 长期改变水文节律与生态系统稳定性的全球变化因素。

nodes.name: 雨燕目
nodes.summary: 雨燕目（Apodiformes）是图谱中的目级分类节点，当前关联177种鸟类，如Starry owlet-nightjar、Wallace's owlet-nightjar、Mountain owlet-nightjar等177种鸟类。

nodes.name: NT
nodes.summary: NT表示 IUCN 近危等级，当前关联914种鸟类，如帝企鹅、小斑几维鸟、大美洲鸵等914种鸟类。

nodes.name: 妆脸蜂鸟
nodes.summary: 妆脸蜂鸟（Hooded visorbearer，学名 Augastes lumachella），分类上属于雨燕目，并归入蜂鸟科，在图谱中标记为近危（NT）物种，主要分布于巴西、迪亞曼蒂納高原、巴伊亞州，常见栖息地包括稀树草原，主要威胁包括栖息地破坏、气候变化。

nodes.name: 蜂鸟科
nodes.summary: 蜂鸟科（Trochilidae）是图谱中的科级分类节点，当前关联90种鸟类，如黑蜂鸟、白颈蜂鸟、火红叉尾蜂鸟等90种鸟类。

nodes.name: 迪亞曼蒂納高原
nodes.summary: 迪亞曼蒂納高原是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联2种鸟类，包括妆脸蜂鸟、Sincorá antwren，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 巴伊亞州
nodes.summary: 巴伊亞州是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联118种鸟类，包括孤䳍、黄腿穴䳍、Pygmy nightjar等118种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

nodes.name: 稀树草原
nodes.summary: 东非常见的开放草地与湿地边缘复合景观。

nodes.name: 巴西
nodes.summary: 巴西是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联854种鸟类，包括大美洲鸵、侏䳍、白腹拟䳍等854种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The hooded visorbearer is endemic to eastern Brazil. Found only in the Chapada Diamantina region of Bahia, it is restricted to campo rupestre, a high-elevation ecoregion that is characterized by poor soils, open vegetation, rocky outcrops and harsh climatic conditions. Its occurs at elevations ranging from , and is considered to be one of South America's obligate grassland birds. It is found in rocky, semi-arid areas with cactus and low shrubs on the summits of mountains and mesas

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: The hooded visorbearer is endemic to eastern Brazil. Found only in the Chapada Diamantina region of Bahia, it is restricted to campo rupestre, a high-elevation ecoregion that is characterized by poor soils, open vegetation, rocky outcrops and harsh climatic conditions. Its occurs at elevations ranging from , and is considered to be one of South America's obligate grassland birds. links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: It is threatened by habitat loss, brought about primarily by unregulated mining and the conversion of the campo to pastureland and human habitation

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: Then, in 1961, Brazilian naturalist Augusto Ruschi led an expedition to an area where they had been collected in the past, and rediscovered them. The International Union for Conservation of Nature now rates the hooded visorbearer as a near-threatened species, based primarily on its relatively small range. The decline of suitable habitat in both size and quality is ongoing, primarily as a result of uncontrolled fires and climate change
